title: [LLVM] Metric added - largest number of basic blocks in a single func…
author: InakiVA
description: This metric gets the size of the biggest count of basic blocks in a single function.

tests.test_body:
; REQUIRES: asserts


define void @foo(i32 %i, i32 %j, i32 %n) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %i, %j
  br i1 %cmp, label %if.then, label %if.end

if.then:
  call void @f()
  br label %if.end

if.end:
  switch i32 %i, label %sw.default [
    i32 1, label %sw.bb
    i32 2, label %sw.bb1
    i32 3, label %sw.bb1
  ]

sw.bb:
  call void @g()
  br label %sw.epilog

sw.bb1:
  call void @h()
  br label %sw.epilog

sw.default:
  call void @k()
  br label %sw.epilog

sw.epilog:
  %cmp2 = icmp sgt i32 %i, %n
  br i1 %cmp2, label %if.then3, label %if.else

if.then3:
  call void @l()
  br label %if.end4

if.else:
  call void @m()
  br label %if.end4

if.end4:
  ret void
}

define void @woo() {
  ret void
}

declare void @f()
declare void @g()
declare void @h()
declare void @k()
declare void @l()
declare void @m()

Will do! I believe the comment was left on a file that didn't end up having any changes after the fixed implementation
